questions.text: What is an embedding in the context of language models?
options: A text encoding that represents tokens as vectors in a high-dimensional space
A layer that adds noise to inputs
questions.correctAnswer: A text encoding that represents tokens as vectors in a high-dimensional space

questions.text: What does it mean if two word embeddings are close together in vector space?
options: They have the same number of letters
They have similar semantic meanings or contexts
They appear in the same sentence
They use the same attention heads
questions.correctAnswer: They have similar semantic meanings or contexts

questions.text: What is an example of word arithmetic in embeddings?
How have word embeddings evolved from simpler approaches like one-hot encoding? What advantages do modern embeddings provide?
What is the embedding dimension of Gemma-4?
What do positional encodings provide to embeddings?
options: Information about token position in the sequence
questions.correctAnswer: Information about token position in the sequence

questions.text: Explain why embeddings are important for understanding how language models process meaning. How do they bridge the gap between discrete text and continuous computation?
